questions.question: What happens when you type the @ key in a Notion page?

options.text: It opens a menu to insert a table.
options.explanation: Inserting a table uses the slash (/) command, not @.

options.text: It displays options to mention a date, person, or page.
options.explanation: The @ key triggers the mention menu, letting you link dates, people, or pages.

options.text: It automatically saves the page as a template.
options.explanation: Saving as a template is done via the ••• menu, not the @ key.

options.text: It creates a new page in the workspace.
options.explanation: Creating a new page uses the slash command or the + button, not @.

questions.question: When you mention a person in a Notion page, how are they notified?

options.text: They receive an email regardless of their notification settings.
options.explanation: Notion respects each user’s notification preferences; email isn’t guaranteed.

options.text: A notification appears in their Notion sidebar inbox.
options.explanation: Mentioning a person sends a notification to their workspace sidebar inbox.

options.text: Their phone vibrates automatically.
options.explanation: Phone vibrations depend on mobile push settings, not a default behavior of mentions.

options.text: They are added as a collaborator to the page.
options.explanation: Mentioning does not change sharing permissions; it only notifies.

questions.question: Which of the following sequences correctly creates a reminder using the @ key?

options.text: @ remind tomorrow at 9am
options.explanation: After typing @, you choose “remind” and then specify the date and time.

options.text: @ date tomorrow then set reminder
options.explanation: You must select the remind option directly; “date” only inserts a timestamp.

options.text: /remind tomorrow 9am
options.explanation: The slash command is for blocks, not for setting reminders.

options.text: @ page then click “Add reminder”
options.explanation: You can’t add a reminder to a page mention; reminders are set via the remind option.

questions.question: In a database, how can you make a reminder include a specific time rather than just a date?

options.text: Enable the “Include time” toggle in the reminder settings.
options.explanation: The article notes that more granular options are available by enabling Include time.

options.text: Add a second @ mention with the time after the date.
options.explanation: Only one reminder can be set per @ mention; the time is controlled by the toggle.

options.text: Create a separate time property in the database.
options.explanation: While you can have a time property, it doesn’t affect the reminder’s precision.

options.text: Set the workspace’s default time zone to the desired time.
options.explanation: Time zones affect display, not the inclusion of a specific time in a reminder.

questions.question: Where do reminders appear once they are triggered?

options.text: In the page’s comment section.
options.explanation: Comments are separate from reminders; reminders go to the inbox.

options.text: In your Notion sidebar’s Inbox.
options.explanation: The article states that reminders are sent to your Inbox in the sidebar.

options.text: As a pop‑up window on the desktop app.
options.explanation: Notion does not use pop‑up windows for reminders; they are delivered via the inbox.

options.text: In a separate “Reminders” page automatically created for you.
options.explanation: No automatic page is generated; reminders are managed through the inbox.

questions.question: Which settings area lets you customize how you receive @mention and reminder notifications?

options.text: Settings → My notifications
options.explanation: The article explicitly mentions that notification settings are under Settings, then My notifications.

options.text: Workspace → Permissions
options.explanation: Permissions control access, not notification preferences.

options.text: Account → Security
options.explanation: Security settings handle login and authentication, not notification delivery.

options.text: Help Center → Customize alerts
options.explanation: The Help Center provides documentation, not a settings screen.

questions.question: What is the primary functional difference between mentioning a page and mentioning a person?

options.text: Mentioning a page creates a clickable link; mentioning a person sends a notification to them.
options.explanation: Pages are linked for navigation, while people are notified via their sidebar inbox.

options.text: Mentioning a page automatically shares the page with the mentioned person.
options.explanation: Linking a page does not change sharing permissions.

options.text: Mentioning a person adds them as an editor of the page.
options.explanation: Mentions do not grant editing rights; they only alert the person.

options.text: Mentioning a page sends an email, whereas mentioning a person does not.
options.explanation: Both mentions follow the user’s notification settings; emails are not guaranteed for either.

questions.question: Can you add a timestamp to a page without creating a reminder, and if so, how?

options.text: Yes, by typing @ followed by a date without selecting the remind option.
options.explanation: The @ menu lets you insert a date (timestamp) directly; a reminder is only added if you choose the remind option.

options.text: No, every date inserted via @ automatically creates a reminder.
options.explanation: Dates can be added as plain timestamps without a reminder.

options.text: Yes, but you must first disable reminders in Settings.
options.explanation: Disabling reminders isn’t required; you simply choose the date option, not remind.

options.text: No, timestamps are only possible through the slash (/) command.
options.explanation: Timestamps are added via the @ menu, not the slash command.
